---
title: "ST606 Project - 6. Combined Model Results"
author: "Kessiya Mary Jose - 25250360"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%X %d %B, %Y')`"
output: html_document
---

#### Setup
```{r setup, message=FALSE, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, cache = TRUE,
                      cache.path = "cache/")
library(tidyverse)
library(rjags)
library(coda)
library(parallel)
library(knitr)

options(mc.cores = 6)
```


#### Loading datasets
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Loading the final dataset
final_data <- read_csv("final_dataset_expanded2.csv",
                       show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    date            = as.Date(paste(year, month, "01", sep = "-")),
    temp_scaled     = as.numeric(scale(mean_temp)),
    rainfall_scaled = as.numeric(scale(rainfall_mm)),
    site            = as.integer(factor(station))
  )

# Creating the three species datasets
fox_data <- final_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(species == "Vulpes vulpes",
                total_sightings >= 1)

hare_data <- final_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(species == "Lepus timidus subsp. hibernicus",
                total_sightings >= 1)

marten_data <- final_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(species == "Martes martes",
                total_sightings >= 1)

cat("Fox rows:", nrow(fox_data), "\n")
cat("Hare rows:", nrow(hare_data), "\n")
cat("Marten rows:", nrow(marten_data), "\n")
```


#### JAGS combined model code
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# This is the combined model that includes all three predictors together:
# 1. Temperature (quadratic with random slopes per site)
# 2. Rainfall (fixed effect)
# 3. Seasonal effects (monthly fixed effects, January as baseline)

jags_combined_code <- "
model {
  for (i in 1:N) {
    y[i] ~ dpois(mu[i]) T(1,)
    log(mu[i]) <- (beta0 + b0[site[i]]) +
                  (beta1 + b1[site[i]]) * temp[i] +
                  (beta2 + b2[site[i]]) * temp[i]^2 +
                  beta3 * rainfall[i] +
                  alpha[month[i]]
  }

  for (s in 1:S) {
    b0[s] ~ dnorm(0, tau0)
    b1[s] ~ dnorm(0, tau1)
    b2[s] ~ dnorm(0, tau2)
  }

  alpha[1] <- 0
  for (m in 2:12) {
    alpha[m] ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  }

  beta0 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta1 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta2 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  beta3 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)

  tau0 ~ dgamma(0.1, 0.1)
  tau1 ~ dgamma(0.1, 0.1)
  tau2 ~ dgamma(0.1, 0.1)

  sigma0 <- 1 / sqrt(tau0)
  sigma1 <- 1 / sqrt(tau1)
  sigma2 <- 1 / sqrt(tau2)
}
"

writeLines(jags_combined_code, "jags_model_combined.txt")
cat("Combined model code saved to jags_model_combined.txt\n")
```


#### Fit combined model - fox
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Fitting the combined model for fox
# Including temperature, rainfall and seasonal effects together

fox_combined_data <- list(
  y        = fox_data$total_sightings,
  temp     = fox_data$temp_scaled,
  rainfall = fox_data$rainfall_scaled,
  month    = fox_data$month,
  site     = fox_data$site,
  N        = nrow(fox_data),
  S        = max(fox_data$site)
)

inits_combined <- list(
  beta0 = 0, beta1 = 0,
  beta2 = 0, beta3 = 0,
  tau0  = 1, tau1  = 1, tau2 = 1
)

set.seed(123)

fox_combined_model <- jags.model(
  file     = "jags_model_combined.txt",
  data     = fox_combined_data,
  inits    = inits_combined,
  n.chains = 3,
  n.adapt  = 1000
)

update(fox_combined_model, n.iter = 5000)

fox_combined_samples <- coda.samples(
  model          = fox_combined_model,
  variable.names = c("beta0", "beta1", "beta2", "beta3",
                     "alpha", "sigma0", "sigma1", "sigma2"),
  n.iter         = 10000,
  thin           = 2
)

cat("Fox combined model complete!\n")
cat("\nConvergence check:\n")
print(gelman.diag(fox_combined_samples[, c("beta0", "beta1",
                                            "beta2", "beta3",
                                            "sigma0", "sigma1",
                                            "sigma2")]))
```


#### Fit combined model - hare
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Fitting the combined model for hare

hare_combined_data <- list(
  y        = hare_data$total_sightings,
  temp     = hare_data$temp_scaled,
  rainfall = hare_data$rainfall_scaled,
  month    = hare_data$month,
  site     = hare_data$site,
  N        = nrow(hare_data),
  S        = max(hare_data$site)
)

set.seed(123)

hare_combined_model <- jags.model(
  file     = "jags_model_combined.txt",
  data     = hare_combined_data,
  inits    = inits_combined,
  n.chains = 3,
  n.adapt  = 1000
)

update(hare_combined_model, n.iter = 8000)

hare_combined_samples <- coda.samples(
  model          = hare_combined_model,
  variable.names = c("beta0", "beta1", "beta2", "beta3",
                     "alpha", "sigma0", "sigma1", "sigma2"),
  n.iter         = 15000,
  thin           = 2
)

cat("Hare combined model complete!\n")
cat("\nConvergence check:\n")
print(gelman.diag(hare_combined_samples[, c("beta0", "beta1",
                                             "beta2", "beta3",
                                             "sigma0", "sigma1",
                                             "sigma2")]))
```


#### Fit combined model - pine marten
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Fitting the combined model for pine marten

marten_combined_data <- list(
  y        = marten_data$total_sightings,
  temp     = marten_data$temp_scaled,
  rainfall = marten_data$rainfall_scaled,
  month    = marten_data$month,
  site     = marten_data$site,
  N        = nrow(marten_data),
  S        = max(marten_data$site)
)

set.seed(123)

marten_combined_model <- jags.model(
  file     = "jags_model_combined.txt",
  data     = marten_combined_data,
  inits    = inits_combined,
  n.chains = 3,
  n.adapt  = 1000
)

update(marten_combined_model, n.iter = 5000)

marten_combined_samples <- coda.samples(
  model          = marten_combined_model,
  variable.names = c("beta0", "beta1", "beta2", "beta3",
                     "alpha", "sigma0", "sigma1", "sigma2"),
  n.iter         = 10000,
  thin           = 2
)

cat("Pine marten combined model complete!\n")
cat("\nConvergence check:\n")
print(gelman.diag(marten_combined_samples[, c("beta0", "beta1",
                                               "beta2", "beta3",
                                               "sigma0", "sigma1",
                                               "sigma2")]))
```


#### Extract combined model results
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extracting results from all three combined models
# Creating clean data frames with posterior means, credible intervals and R-hat

# Function to extract results neatly
extract_results <- function(samples, species_name) {
  s <- summary(samples)
  rhat <- gelman.diag(samples[, c("beta0", "beta1", "beta2", "beta3",
                                   "sigma0", "sigma1", "sigma2")])

  # Main parameters only (not alpha)
  params <- c("beta0", "beta1", "beta2", "beta3",
              "sigma0", "sigma1", "sigma2")

  data.frame(
    species       = species_name,
    parameter     = params,
    posterior_mean = round(s$statistics[params, "Mean"], 4),
    sd            = round(s$statistics[params, "SD"], 4),
    lower_95      = round(s$quantiles[params, "2.5%"], 4),
    upper_95      = round(s$quantiles[params, "97.5%"], 4),
    rhat          = round(rhat$psrf[params, "Point est."], 3)
  )
}

fox_combined_results    <- extract_results(fox_combined_samples,
                                           "Vulpes vulpes (Fox)")
hare_combined_results   <- extract_results(hare_combined_samples,
                                           "Lepus timidus (Hare)")
marten_combined_results <- extract_results(marten_combined_samples,
                                           "Martes martes (Pine marten)")

# Combining all three into one table
all_combined_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fox_combined_results,
  hare_combined_results,
  marten_combined_results
)

cat("Combined model coefficient table:\n")
print(all_combined_results)

write_csv(all_combined_results, "combined_model_coefficients.csv")
cat("\nSaved: combined_model_coefficients.csv\n")
```


#### Coefficient table - all species combined
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Combining all three species results into one clean table
all_combined_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fox_combined_results,
  hare_combined_results,
  marten_combined_results
) %>%
  # removing the ugly row index from parameter column
  dplyr::mutate(parameter = gsub("\\.\\.\\..*", "", parameter))

write_csv(all_combined_results, "combined_model_coefficients.csv")
cat("Saved: combined_model_coefficients.csv\n")
```


#### Dissertation coefficient table
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(knitr)

param_labels <- c(
  "beta0"  = "Intercept (β₀)",
  "beta1"  = "Temperature linear (β₁)",
  "beta2"  = "Temperature quadratic (β₂)",
  "beta3"  = "Rainfall (β₃)",
  "sigma0" = "SD random intercept (σ₀)",
  "sigma1" = "SD random temp slope (σ₁)",
  "sigma2" = "SD random quad slope (σ₂)"
)

dissertation_table <- all_combined_results %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Parameter = param_labels[parameter],
    CI_95     = paste0("(", lower_95, ", ", upper_95, ")")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Species       = species,
    Parameter,
    `Post. Mean`  = posterior_mean,
    `95% CI`      = CI_95,
    `R-hat`       = rhat
  )

write_csv(dissertation_table, "dissertation_coefficient_table.csv")

# To displaynice formatted table in the knitted HTML
kable(dissertation_table,
      caption = "Table 1: Combined model coefficient estimates - posterior means, 95% credible intervals and R-hat convergence diagnostics",
      align   = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r"),
      row.names = FALSE)
```
